task_construct: critical-object identity and coordinate-wise ordinal risk relation over a declared deterministic ego corridor
scope: designed-range ordinal causal consistency, not calibrated real-world risk
primary_receiver_summary: per-contrast counts of expected, reversal, and unavailable common valid timestamps
gates: all five outputs are new and complete 36 of 36 steps
exact timestamp, ego, plan, and action pairing
all scored frames have complete future actor history
independent oriented-footprint and swept-corridor recomputation preserves the intended contrast

decision_rules.accepted: geometry gate passes, every planned receiver timestamp is available, and the claimed ordinal relation has zero reversals
decision_rules.down-weighted: geometry is valid and aggregate direction is expected, but one or more receiver timestamps are unavailable or reversed
decision_rules.rejected: the claimed relation is contradicted by independent geometry, aggregate receiver direction reverses, or an identity/horizon gate fails

occlusion.reason: pixel masking would test receiver preprocessing; a second actor would confound actor count and interaction structure
occlusion.allowed_observation: report natural visibility differences without causal occlusion claims

stop_rules: do not change positions, speed, threshold, frame range, association rule, or expected order after inspecting output
stop after one complete five-run matrix regardless of result
retain early termination as failed evidence and do not replace it under audit 001
any manifest or config change requires audit 002 and new hashes
strongest_allowed_claim: within the declared 2x2 design range and complete-future window, the HUGSIM intervention satisfies independently recomputed geometric order and the frozen Sparse4Dv3 probe preserves the predeclared longitudinal and lateral ordinal relations without reversal
forbidden_claims: the factor levels are real-world safe or unsafe thresholds
the scripted actor is a realistic traffic policy
the result proves real AD action, collision risk, real-sim equivalence, or general HUGSIM fitness
